data.description: This is a partial rule describing the concentration mechanic, likely continuing on page 263.
An instantaneous spell harms, heals, creates, or alters a location, object, or creature for just one round. The spell energy erupts the moment you cast the spell and then ceases to function.
Casts the Heal spell (save DC 30) on up to six creatures of your choice within range, each of which must make a Wisdom saving throw.
Casts Healing Word on up to six willing targets within range. Each target regains 105 + your spellcasting ability modifier hit points.
An instantaneous spell instantly takes effect, then ends. It cannot be dispelled and neither it nor its effects can be suppressed by any form of magic immunity.

This is a flask of thick, sticky liquid that can be used to douse a target in an area up to 5 feet in diameter. When the oil ignites, it deals 1d4 fire damage to all creatures in the area and on each creature’s turn, they must make a Dexterity saving throw or take 1d4 fire damage.
